content.json:
# 🤖 Azure Arc Machine Intelligence Centre

## **Your Complete Single-Machine Dashboard**

**🎯 What This Does**: Get a complete 360-degree view of any Azure Arc machine in your organisation with just one click.

**✨ What You'll See:**
- **🔍 Live Performance Data** - CPU, memory, disk, and network activity
- **🛡️ Security Health** - Defender alerts, threats, and security status
- **� Vulnerability Intelligence** - Real CVSS risk scores and detailed security assessments
- **📊 Security Health Summary** - Complete security report card with compliance scores
- **�💾 Software Tracking** - Recent installs, removals, and change history
- **🗄️ SQL Server Details** - Database inventory, performance, and security (when available)
- **⚙️ Arc Management** - Extensions, policies, and compliance status
- **📊 Operations Timeline** - Connection history, updates, and maintenance events

**🚀 Why This Matters**: This dashboard shows how Azure Arc transforms any machine into a fully managed, monitored, and secured part of your cloud environment with real-time vulnerability intelligence.

---

### 📋 **Choose Your Arc Machine**

parameters.name: SelectedMachine
parameters.label: 🖥️ Arc Machine
parameters.query:
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines'
| project label = name, value = name, group = resourceGroup, subscription = subscriptionId
| order by label asc

content.json:
## 🏷️ **Machine Overview & Identity**

**What You're Looking At**: Basic machine information pulled directly from Azure Arc registration and connection data.

**Why This Matters**: Quickly check the machine's identity, location, and whether the Arc agent is working properly before looking at detailed information.

content.query:
// Machine Overview - Basic Identity and Status
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines'
| where name =~ '{SelectedMachine}'
| extend 
    MachineName = name,
    OperatingSystem = tostring(properties.osName),
    OSVersion = tostring(properties.osVersion),
    AgentVersion = tostring(properties.agentVersion),
    LastStatusChange = todatetime(properties.lastStatusChange),
    Status = tostring(properties.status),
    DomainName = tostring(properties.domainName),
    Location = location,
    ResourceGroup = resourceGroup,
    Subscription = subscriptionId,
    PrivateIP = tostring(properties.networkProfile.publicIpAddresses[0]),
    FQDN = tostring(properties.dnsFqdn),
    LogicalCores = toint(properties.detectedProperties.logicalCoreCount),
    TotalMemoryGB = toint(properties.detectedProperties.totalPhysicalMemoryInGigabytes),
    Manufacturer = tostring(properties.detectedProperties.manufacturer),
    Model = tostring(properties.detectedProperties.model)
| extend 
    DaysOffline = case(
        Status == "Connected", 0,
        Status == "Disconnected", datetime_diff('day', now(), LastStatusChange),
        datetime_diff('day', now(), LastStatusChange)
    )
| extend 
    HealthStatus = case(
        Status == "Connected", "🟢 Healthy",
        Status == "Disconnected" and DaysOffline <= 1, "🟡 Recent Disconnect",
        Status == "Disconnected" and DaysOffline <= 7, "🟠 Short Disconnect",
        Status == "Disconnected", "🔴 Extended Offline",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    ),
    HardwareSpecs = case(
        LogicalCores > 0 and TotalMemoryGB > 0, strcat(LogicalCores, " cores, ", TotalMemoryGB, " GB RAM"),
        LogicalCores > 0, strcat(LogicalCores, " cores"),
        TotalMemoryGB > 0, strcat(TotalMemoryGB, " GB RAM"),
        "Hardware info not available"
    ),
    MachineType = case(
        isnotempty(Manufacturer) and isnotempty(Model), strcat(Manufacturer, " ", Model),
        isnotempty(Manufacturer), Manufacturer,
        "Physical/Virtual Machine"
    )
| project 
    MachineName, HealthStatus, OperatingSystem, OSVersion, 
    AgentVersion, Status, LastStatusChange, DaysOffline,
    HardwareSpecs, MachineType, DomainName, Location, ResourceGroup, PrivateIP, FQDN
content.title: 🖥️ Machine Identity & Arc Agent Status
content.description: Core machine information and Azure Arc connectivity status

content.json:
## 📊 **Performance Intelligence**

**What You're Looking At**: Live and historical performance data collected by Azure Monitor from this specific machine.

**Why This Matters**: Understand how your machine is performing right now, spot potential problems early, and plan for future capacity needs.

content.query:
// Performance Hero KPIs - Current Status
InsightsMetrics
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where (Namespace == "Processor" and Name == "UtilizationPercentage") or
        (Namespace == "Memory" and Name == "AvailableMB") or
        (Namespace == "LogicalDisk" and Name == "FreeSpacePercentage")
| summarize 
    LatestValue = avg(Val),
    SampleCount = count()
    by Namespace, Name
| extend 
    MetricName = case(
        Name == "UtilizationPercentage", "CPU Usage",
        Name == "AvailableMB", "Available Memory (MB)",
        Name == "FreeSpacePercentage", "Disk Free Space",
        Name
    ),
    Status = case(
        Name == "UtilizationPercentage" and LatestValue > 80, "🔴 High",
        Name == "UtilizationPercentage" and LatestValue > 60, "🟡 Moderate", 
        Name == "UtilizationPercentage", "🟢 Normal",
        Name == "AvailableMB" and LatestValue < 1024, "🔴 Low",
        Name == "AvailableMB" and LatestValue < 2048, "🟡 Moderate",
        Name == "AvailableMB", "🟢 Good",
        Name == "FreeSpacePercentage" and LatestValue < 10, "🔴 Critical",
        Name == "FreeSpacePercentage" and LatestValue < 20, "🟡 Low",
        Name == "FreeSpacePercentage", "🟢 Good",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    ),
    FormattedValue = case(
        Name == "UtilizationPercentage", strcat(round(LatestValue, 1), "%"),
        Name == "AvailableMB", strcat(round(LatestValue, 0), " MB"),
        Name == "FreeSpacePercentage", strcat(round(LatestValue, 1), "%"),
        tostring(round(LatestValue, 2))
    )
| project MetricName, Status, FormattedValue, SampleCount
content.title: ⚡ Real-Time Performance Metrics (Last Hour)
content.description: Current CPU, memory, and disk usage with health status indicators

content.query:
// CPU Usage Trend - Historical Data
InsightsMetrics
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where Namespace == "Processor" and Name == "UtilizationPercentage"
| summarize CPUUsage = avg(Val) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| extend 
    CPUStatus = case(
        CPUUsage > 80, "High",
        CPUUsage > 60, "Moderate",
        "Normal"
    )
| project TimeGenerated, CPUUsage, CPUStatus
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: 🖥️ CPU Usage Trend Over Time
content.description: Historical CPU utilisation percentage - helps identify performance patterns and peak usage periods

content.query:
// Memory Usage Trend - Historical Data
InsightsMetrics
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where Namespace == "Memory" and Name == "AvailableMB"
| summarize MemoryAvailableMB = avg(Val) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| extend 
    MemoryAvailableGB = round(MemoryAvailableMB / 1024.0, 2),
    MemoryStatus = case(
        MemoryAvailableMB < 1024, "Critical",
        MemoryAvailableMB < 2048, "Low",
        MemoryAvailableMB < 4096, "Moderate",
        "Healthy"
    )
| project TimeGenerated, MemoryAvailableGB, MemoryStatus
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: 💾 Memory Availability Trend Over Time
content.description: Historical available memory in GB - shows memory consumption patterns and helps plan capacity

content.query:
// Disk Free Space Trend - Historical Data
InsightsMetrics
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where Namespace == "LogicalDisk" and Name == "FreeSpacePercentage"
| summarize DiskFreeSpace = avg(Val) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| extend 
    DiskStatus = case(
        DiskFreeSpace < 10, "Critical",
        DiskFreeSpace < 20, "Low",
        DiskFreeSpace < 40, "Moderate",
        "Healthy"
    )
| project TimeGenerated, DiskFreeSpace, DiskStatus
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: � Disk Free Space Trend Over Time
content.description: Historical disk free space percentage - tracks storage consumption and helps prevent disk full situations

content.json:
### 📊 **Performance Pattern Analysis**

**What These Tables Show**: The next three tables break down your machine's performance into different ranges (called 'buckets') and show you what percentage of time your machine operates in each range.

**Why This Helps**: Instead of just seeing averages, you can see the full picture - for example, if your CPU averages 50%, these tables tell you whether it's consistently at 50% or bouncing between 20% and 80%. This helps you plan for capacity and spot performance patterns.

content.query:
// Performance Distribution Analysis - CPU Usage Buckets
let CPUData = 
    InsightsMetrics
    | where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
    | where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
    | where Namespace == "Processor" and Name == "UtilizationPercentage"
    | extend CPUUsage = round(Val, 1)
    | summarize TotalCPUSamples = count();
InsightsMetrics
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where Namespace == "Processor" and Name == "UtilizationPercentage"
| extend CPUUsage = round(Val, 1)
| extend 
    UsageBucket = case(
        CPUUsage >= 0 and CPUUsage <= 10, "0-10%",
        CPUUsage > 10 and CPUUsage <= 20, "11-20%",
        CPUUsage > 20 and CPUUsage <= 30, "21-30%",
        CPUUsage > 30 and CPUUsage <= 40, "31-40%",
        CPUUsage > 40 and CPUUsage <= 50, "41-50%",
        CPUUsage > 50 and CPUUsage <= 60, "51-60%",
        CPUUsage > 60 and CPUUsage <= 70, "61-70%",
        CPUUsage > 70 and CPUUsage <= 80, "71-80%",
        CPUUsage > 80 and CPUUsage <= 90, "81-90%",
        CPUUsage > 90 and CPUUsage <= 100, "91-100%",
        "Other"
    ),
    BucketOrder = case(
        CPUUsage >= 0 and CPUUsage <= 10, 1,
        CPUUsage > 10 and CPUUsage <= 20, 2,
        CPUUsage > 20 and CPUUsage <= 30, 3,
        CPUUsage > 30 and CPUUsage <= 40, 4,
        CPUUsage > 40 and CPUUsage <= 50, 5,
        CPUUsage > 50 and CPUUsage <= 60, 6,
        CPUUsage > 60 and CPUUsage <= 70, 7,
        CPUUsage > 70 and CPUUsage <= 80, 8,
        CPUUsage > 80 and CPUUsage <= 90, 9,
        CPUUsage > 90 and CPUUsage <= 100, 10,
        11
    )
| summarize SampleCount = count() by UsageBucket, BucketOrder
| extend 
    TotalSamples = toint(toscalar(CPUData | project TotalCPUSamples)),
    PercentageOfTime = round(100.0 * SampleCount / toint(toscalar(CPUData | project TotalCPUSamples)), 2),
    PerformanceZone = case(
        UsageBucket in ("0-10%", "11-20%", "21-30%"), "🟢 Optimal",
        UsageBucket in ("31-40%", "41-50%", "51-60%"), "🟡 Normal",
        UsageBucket in ("61-70%", "71-80%"), "🟠 Busy",
        UsageBucket in ("81-90%", "91-100%"), "🔴 Stressed",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    )
| project 
    CPURange = UsageBucket,
    PerformanceZone,
    SampleCount,
    PercentageOfTime,
    TotalSamples,
    BucketOrder
| order by BucketOrder asc
content.title: 📊 CPU Usage Distribution Analysis
content.description: Shows how often your CPU runs at different usage levels - helps you understand if your machine typically runs smoothly (0-30%), normally busy (31-60%), or frequently stressed (61-100%). Each row shows what percentage of time your CPU spent in that usage range.

content.query:
// Performance Distribution Analysis - Memory Usage Buckets
let MemoryData = 
    InsightsMetrics
    | where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
    | where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
    | where Namespace == "Memory" and Name == "AvailableMB"
    | extend MemoryAvailableGB = round(Val / 1024.0, 1)
    | summarize TotalMemorySamples = count();
InsightsMetrics
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where Namespace == "Memory" and Name == "AvailableMB"
| extend MemoryAvailableGB = round(Val / 1024.0, 1)
| extend 
    MemoryBucket = case(
        MemoryAvailableGB >= 0 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 1, "0-1 GB",
        MemoryAvailableGB > 1 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 2, "1-2 GB",
        MemoryAvailableGB > 2 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 4, "2-4 GB",
        MemoryAvailableGB > 4 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 8, "4-8 GB",
        MemoryAvailableGB > 8 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 16, "8-16 GB",
        MemoryAvailableGB > 16 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 32, "16-32 GB",
        MemoryAvailableGB > 32 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 64, "32-64 GB",
        MemoryAvailableGB > 64, "64+ GB",
        "Other"
    ),
    BucketOrder = case(
        MemoryAvailableGB >= 0 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 1, 1,
        MemoryAvailableGB > 1 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 2, 2,
        MemoryAvailableGB > 2 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 4, 3,
        MemoryAvailableGB > 4 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 8, 4,
        MemoryAvailableGB > 8 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 16, 5,
        MemoryAvailableGB > 16 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 32, 6,
        MemoryAvailableGB > 32 and MemoryAvailableGB <= 64, 7,
        MemoryAvailableGB > 64, 8,
        9
    )
| summarize SampleCount = count() by MemoryBucket, BucketOrder
| extend 
    TotalSamples = toint(toscalar(MemoryData | project TotalMemorySamples)),
    PercentageOfTime = round(100.0 * SampleCount / toint(toscalar(MemoryData | project TotalMemorySamples)), 2),
    MemoryHealthZone = case(
        MemoryBucket in ("0-1 GB", "1-2 GB"), "🔴 Critical",
        MemoryBucket in ("2-4 GB"), "🟠 Low",
        MemoryBucket in ("4-8 GB", "8-16 GB"), "🟡 Moderate",
        MemoryBucket in ("16-32 GB", "32-64 GB", "64+ GB"), "🟢 Abundant",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    )
| project 
    MemoryRange = MemoryBucket,
    MemoryHealthZone,
    SampleCount,
    PercentageOfTime,
    TotalSamples,
    BucketOrder
| order by BucketOrder asc
content.title: 💾 Memory Availability Distribution Analysis
content.description: Shows how much free memory your machine typically has available - helps you spot if you're often running low on memory (red zones) or have plenty to spare (green zones). Each row shows what percentage of time your machine had that amount of free memory.

content.query:
// Performance Distribution Analysis - Disk Space Buckets
let DiskData = 
    InsightsMetrics
    | where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
    | where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
    | where Namespace == "LogicalDisk" and Name == "FreeSpacePercentage"
    | extend DiskFreePercent = round(Val, 1)
    | summarize TotalDiskSamples = count();
InsightsMetrics
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where Namespace == "LogicalDisk" and Name == "FreeSpacePercentage"
| extend DiskFreePercent = round(Val, 1)
| extend 
    DiskBucket = case(
        DiskFreePercent >= 0 and DiskFreePercent <= 10, "0-10%",
        DiskFreePercent > 10 and DiskFreePercent <= 20, "11-20%",
        DiskFreePercent > 20 and DiskFreePercent <= 30, "21-30%",
        DiskFreePercent > 30 and DiskFreePercent <= 40, "31-40%",
        DiskFreePercent > 40 and DiskFreePercent <= 50, "41-50%",
        DiskFreePercent > 50 and DiskFreePercent <= 60, "51-60%",
        DiskFreePercent > 60 and DiskFreePercent <= 70, "61-70%",
        DiskFreePercent > 70 and DiskFreePercent <= 80, "71-80%",
        DiskFreePercent > 80 and DiskFreePercent <= 90, "81-90%",
        DiskFreePercent > 90 and DiskFreePercent <= 100, "91-100%",
        "Other"
    ),
    BucketOrder = case(
        DiskFreePercent >= 0 and DiskFreePercent <= 10, 1,
        DiskFreePercent > 10 and DiskFreePercent <= 20, 2,
        DiskFreePercent > 20 and DiskFreePercent <= 30, 3,
        DiskFreePercent > 30 and DiskFreePercent <= 40, 4,
        DiskFreePercent > 40 and DiskFreePercent <= 50, 5,
        DiskFreePercent > 50 and DiskFreePercent <= 60, 6,
        DiskFreePercent > 60 and DiskFreePercent <= 70, 7,
        DiskFreePercent > 70 and DiskFreePercent <= 80, 8,
        DiskFreePercent > 80 and DiskFreePercent <= 90, 9,
        DiskFreePercent > 90 and DiskFreePercent <= 100, 10,
        11
    )
| summarize SampleCount = count() by DiskBucket, BucketOrder
| extend 
    TotalSamples = toint(toscalar(DiskData | project TotalDiskSamples)),
    PercentageOfTime = round(100.0 * SampleCount / toint(toscalar(DiskData | project TotalDiskSamples)), 2),
    DiskHealthZone = case(
        DiskBucket in ("0-10%", "11-20%"), "🔴 Critical",
        DiskBucket in ("21-30%", "31-40%"), "🟠 Low Space",
        DiskBucket in ("41-50%", "51-60%"), "🟡 Moderate",
        DiskBucket in ("61-70%", "71-80%", "81-90%", "91-100%"), "🟢 Healthy",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    )
| project 
    DiskFreeRange = DiskBucket,
    DiskHealthZone,
    SampleCount,
    PercentageOfTime,
    TotalSamples,
    BucketOrder
| order by BucketOrder asc
content.title: 💿 Disk Free Space Distribution Analysis
content.description: Shows how much free disk space your machine usually has - helps you see if you're often running low on storage (red zones) or have healthy amounts of free space (green zones). Each row shows what percentage of time your disk had that amount of free space available.

content.json:
## 🛡️ **Security & Defender Intelligence**

**What You're Looking At**: Microsoft Defender for Cloud security assessments, recommendations, and alerts for this Arc machine.

**Why This Matters**: See your security status, get specific recommendations to improve it, and check compliance through Defender for Cloud's comprehensive security monitoring.

### **🎯 How to Read the Security Assessment Tile:**

**📊 The Big Number (Top)**: Your security compliance score (0-100%)
- Shows what percentage of security recommendations are currently "Healthy"
- **Higher percentage = Better security** (90%+ is excellent, below 70% needs attention)

**� The Small Number (Bottom)**: Count of **High Severity** security issues
- This is the number of critical security problems found
- **Lower number = Better security** (0 is ideal, 3+ means urgent action needed)

**🎨 Tile Colour & Status Text Meanings**:
- 🟢 **Green "Healthy"**: No high-severity issues, good security posture
- 🟡 **Yellow "Multiple Medium Issues"**: Some medium-priority items to address
- 🟠 **Orange "Some Medium Issues"**: Few medium-priority concerns
- 🔴 **Red "Critical Issues"**: High-severity security problems requiring immediate action
- ⚪ **Grey "No Assessments Found"**: Defender for Cloud not configured or no data

**💡 Example**: If you see a red tile showing "Critical Issues" with score 65% and small number 3, this means:
- 65% of security checks are passing (the big number)
- You have 3 high-severity security problems (the small number)
- The red colour warns you about those 3 critical issues that need immediate attention

content.query:
// Defender for Cloud - Security Recommendations for Arc VM
// Using the corrected schema structure from real data analysis
SecurityResources
| where type == "microsoft.security/assessments"
| extend 
    ResourceType = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceType),
    ResourceName = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceName),
    NativeResourceId = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.NativeResourceId),
    Recommendation = tostring(properties.displayName),
    Status = tostring(properties.status.code),
    Severity = tostring(properties.metadata.severity),
    Category = tostring(properties.metadata.categories[0])
| where ResourceType == "virtualMachines" or NativeResourceId contains "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines"
| extend 
    MachineName = case(
        NativeResourceId contains "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines", tostring(split(NativeResourceId, '/')[8]),
        ResourceType == "virtualMachines", ResourceName,
        "Unknown"
    )
| where MachineName =~ '{SelectedMachine}'
| summarize 
    TotalRecommendations = count(),
    HighSeverity = countif(Severity == "High"),
    MediumSeverity = countif(Severity == "Medium"),
    LowSeverity = countif(Severity == "Low"),
    HealthyCount = countif(Status == "Healthy"),
    UnhealthyCount = countif(Status == "Unhealthy")
| extend 
    SecurityScore = case(
        TotalRecommendations > 0, round(100.0 * HealthyCount / TotalRecommendations, 0),
        100.0
    ),
    SecurityScoreDisplay = case(
        TotalRecommendations > 0, strcat(tostring(round(100.0 * HealthyCount / TotalRecommendations, 0)), "%"),
        "100%"
    ),
    SecurityStatus = case(
        TotalRecommendations == 0, "⚪ No Assessments Found",
        HighSeverity > 0, "🔴 Critical Issues",
        MediumSeverity > 3, "🟡 Multiple Medium Issues",
        MediumSeverity > 0, "🟠 Some Medium Issues",
        LowSeverity > 0, "💙 Minor Issues Only",
        "🟢 Healthy"
    )
| project SecurityStatus, SecurityScoreDisplay, SecurityScore, TotalRecommendations, HighSeverity, MediumSeverity, LowSeverity, HealthyCount, UnhealthyCount
content.title: 🛡️ Defender for Cloud - Security Assessments Summary
content.description: Microsoft Defender for Servers security recommendations and compliance status for this machine

content.query:
// Security Assessment Discovery - Find Arc Machine Assessments
// Based on the schema analysis, look for Arc machine-specific assessments
SecurityResources
| where type == "microsoft.security/assessments"
| extend 
    ResourceType = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceType),
    ResourceName = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceName),
    ResourceId = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceId),
    NativeResourceId = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.NativeResourceId)
| where ResourceType == "virtualMachines" or NativeResourceId contains "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines"
| extend 
    MachineName = case(
        NativeResourceId contains "microsoft.hybridcompute/machines", tostring(split(NativeResourceId, '/')[8]),
        ResourceType == "virtualMachines", ResourceName,
        "Unknown"
    )
| where MachineName =~ '{SelectedMachine}'
| project 
    MachineName,
    ResourceType,
    AssessmentName = tostring(properties.displayName),
    Status = tostring(properties.status.code),
    Severity = tostring(properties.metadata.severity),
    Category = tostring(properties.metadata.categories[0]),
    Description = tostring(properties.metadata.description)
| take 10
content.title: � Security Schema Exploration - What Data Exists?
content.description: Find security assessments specifically for the chosen Arc machine using the correct data structure

content.json:
## 💾 **Software Intelligence & Change Tracking**

**What You're Looking At**: Database detection (SQL Server, PostgreSQL, MySQL), software change tracking, and installation history.

**Why This Matters**: Find databases for licence optimisation, track software changes for compliance, and spot potential security risks from unauthorised installations.

content.query:
// Database Discovery - SQL Server, PostgreSQL, MySQL Detection
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines'
| where name =~ '{SelectedMachine}'
| extend 
    machineName = name,
    status = tostring(properties.status),
    mssqlInstalled = coalesce(tobool(properties.detectedProperties.mssqldiscovered), false),
    pgsqlInstalled = coalesce(tobool(properties.detectedProperties.pgsqldiscovered), false),
    mysqlInstalled = coalesce(tobool(properties.detectedProperties.mysqldiscovered), false),
    osName = tostring(properties.osName),
    coreCount = tostring(properties.detectedProperties.logicalCoreCount),
    totalMemoryGB = tostring(properties.detectedProperties.totalPhysicalMemoryInGigabytes)
| extend 
    DatabasesFound = case(
        mssqlInstalled and pgsqlInstalled and mysqlInstalled, "🗄️ SQL Server + PostgreSQL + MySQL",
        mssqlInstalled and pgsqlInstalled, "🗄️ SQL Server + PostgreSQL",
        mssqlInstalled and mysqlInstalled, "🗄️ SQL Server + MySQL", 
        pgsqlInstalled and mysqlInstalled, "🗄️ PostgreSQL + MySQL",
        mssqlInstalled, "🗄️ SQL Server Only",
        pgsqlInstalled, "🐘 PostgreSQL Only",
        mysqlInstalled, "🐬 MySQL Only",
        "❌ No Databases Detected"
    ),
    DatabaseCount = case(
        mssqlInstalled and pgsqlInstalled and mysqlInstalled, 3,
        (mssqlInstalled and pgsqlInstalled) or (mssqlInstalled and mysqlInstalled) or (pgsqlInstalled and mysqlInstalled), 2,
        mssqlInstalled or pgsqlInstalled or mysqlInstalled, 1,
        0
    )
| project machineName, DatabasesFound, DatabaseCount, osName, coreCount, totalMemoryGB, mssqlInstalled, pgsqlInstalled, mysqlInstalled
content.title: 🗄️ Database Detection & Discovery
content.description: Automatically detected database engines on this Arc machine

content.query:
// Recent Software Changes - Simple approach
ConfigurationChange
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where ConfigChangeType == "Software"
| where ChangeCategory in ("Added", "Removed")
| project 
    TimeGenerated,
    ChangeType = ChangeCategory,
    SoftwareName,
    Publisher,
    Previous
| extend 
    ChangeIcon = case(
        ChangeType == "Added", "➕ Installed",
        ChangeType == "Removed", "➖ Removed",
        ChangeType
    ),
    Version = case(
        isnotempty(Previous) and Previous != "Not Installed", Previous,
        "Unknown"
    )
| project TimeGenerated, ChangeIcon, SoftwareName, Publisher, Version
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 20
content.title: 📦 Recent Software Changes (Last 20)
content.description: Software installations and removals tracked by Change Tracking & Inventory

content.json:
## 🗄️ **SQL Server Intelligence** (When Available)

**What You're Looking At**: Detailed insights into SQL Server instances running on this Arc machine, including databases, performance, and security assessments.

**Why This Matters**: Gives database administrators critical information about SQL health, security vulnerabilities, and performance optimisation opportunities.

content.query:
// SQL Server Instance Overview
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.azurearcdata/sqlserverinstances'
| where id contains '{SelectedMachine}'
| extend 
    InstanceName = name,
    Version = tostring(properties.version),
    Edition = tostring(properties.edition),
    Status = tostring(properties.status),
    LicenseType = tostring(properties.licenseType),
    Cores = toint(properties.cores),
    HostType = tostring(properties.hostType)
| extend 
    StatusIcon = case(
        Status == "Connected", "🟢 Connected",
        Status == "Disconnected", "� Disconnected",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    ),
    LicenseIcon = case(
        LicenseType == "Paid", "� Licensed",
        LicenseType == "Free", "🆓 Free",
        LicenseType == "HADR", "🔄 HADR",
        "❓ Unknown"
    )
| project 
    InstanceName,
    StatusIcon,
    Version,
    Edition,
    LicenseIcon,
    Cores,
    HostType
| order by InstanceName asc
content.title: 🏢 SQL Server Instances Overview
content.description: SQL Server instances discovered and managed by Azure Arc

content.query:
// SQL Server Database Discovery with Enhanced Details
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.azurearcdata/sqlserverinstances/databases'
| where id contains '{SelectedMachine}'
| extend 
    DatabaseName = name,
    State = tostring(properties.state),
    ProvisioningState = tostring(properties.provisioningState),
    SizeMB = todouble(properties.sizeMB),
    DataFileSizeMB = todouble(properties.dataFileSizeMB),
    LogFileSizeMB = todouble(properties.logFileSizeMB),
    SpaceAvailableMB = todouble(properties.spaceAvailableMB),
    IsEncrypted = tobool(properties.databaseOptions.isEncrypted),
    IsReadOnly = tobool(properties.isReadOnly),
    IsAutoShrinkOn = tobool(properties.databaseOptions.isAutoShrinkOn),
    IsAutoCloseOn = tobool(properties.databaseOptions.isAutoCloseOn),
    IsTrustworthyOn = tobool(properties.databaseOptions.isTrustworthyOn),
    IsMemoryOptimizationEnabled = tobool(properties.databaseOptions.isMemoryOptimizationEnabled),
    CompatibilityLevel = toint(properties.compatibilityLevel),
    Collation = tostring(properties.collationName),
    RecoveryMode = tostring(properties.recoveryMode),
    CreateDate = todatetime(properties.databaseCreationDate),
    LastUploadTime = todatetime(properties.lastDatabaseUploadTime),
    AssessmentUploadTime = todatetime(properties.migration.assessment.assessmentUploadTime)
| extend 
    SizeGB = round(SizeMB / 1024.0, 2),
    DataSizeGB = round(DataFileSizeMB / 1024.0, 2),
    LogSizeGB = round(LogFileSizeMB / 1024.0, 2),
    FreeSpaceGB = round(SpaceAvailableMB / 1024.0, 2),
    StatusIcon = case(
        State == "Online", "🟢 Online",
        State == "Offline", "🔴 Offline",
        State == "Restoring", "🟡 Restoring",
        State == "Recovering", "🟡 Recovering",
        State == "Suspect", "🔴 Suspect",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    ),
    EncryptionStatus = case(
        IsEncrypted == true, "🔒 Encrypted",
        IsEncrypted == false, "🔓 Not Encrypted",
        "❓ Unknown"
    ),
    ReadOnlyStatus = case(
        IsReadOnly == true, "📖 Read-Only",
        "✏️ Read-Write"
    ),
    ConfigurationFlags = strcat(
        case(IsAutoShrinkOn == true, "⚠️AutoShrink ", ""),
        case(IsAutoCloseOn == true, "⚠️AutoClose ", ""),
        case(IsTrustworthyOn == true, "�Trustworthy ", ""),
        case(IsMemoryOptimizationEnabled == true, "⚡InMemory ", "")
    ),
    DaysOld = datetime_diff('day', now(), CreateDate),
    DaysSinceLastUpload = datetime_diff('day', now(), LastUploadTime),
    SpaceUtilization = case(
        SizeMB > 0 and SpaceAvailableMB >= 0, round(100.0 * (SizeMB - SpaceAvailableMB) / SizeMB, 1),
        0.0
    )
| project 
    DatabaseName,
    StatusIcon,
    SizeGB,
    DataSizeGB,
    LogSizeGB,
    FreeSpaceGB,
    SpaceUtilization,
    EncryptionStatus,
    ReadOnlyStatus,
    RecoveryMode,
    CompatibilityLevel,
    ConfigurationFlags,
    DaysOld,
    DaysSinceLastUpload
| order by SizeGB desc
content.title: 🗄️ SQL Server Databases - Comprehensive Analysis
content.description: Detailed database inventory including storage breakdown, configuration flags, encryption, and space usage

content.json:
## 🛡️ **Vulnerability Intelligence**

**What You're Looking At**: Real security vulnerability data from Microsoft Defender showing exactly what security holes exist on this machine.

**Why This Matters**: This shows you the actual security threats on your machine with clear risk scores. You'll know exactly what needs fixing first and how urgent each problem is.

### **🎯 How to Read the Vulnerability Levels:**

**🔴 Critical (9.0+)**: **Drop everything and fix now** - These can be hacked easily
**🟠 High (7.0-8.9)**: **Fix this week** - Serious security risks that need quick action  
**🟡 Medium (4.0-6.9)**: **Fix next month** - Important but not urgent
**🟢 Low (0.1-3.9)**: **Fix when convenient** - Minor security issues
**⚪ Scanner Not Set Up**: **Install security scanner first** - We can't check for problems yet
**✅ No Vulnerabilities**: **Well done!** - Your machine is secure

### **💡 Real Example:**
If you see "🔴 Critical (9.8)" that means hackers can easily break into this machine. Fix it immediately before continuing with other work.

content.query:
// DETAILED VULNERABILITY STATUS TABLE
SecurityResources  
| where type == "microsoft.security/assessments"
| where properties.displayName == "Machines should have vulnerability findings resolved"
| extend 
    ResourceId = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceId),
    ResourceName = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceName)
| where ResourceId contains "hybridcompute"
| where ResourceName == "{SelectedMachine}"
| extend 
    Status = tostring(properties.status.code),
    MaxCVSS = tostring(properties.additionalData.maxCvss30Score),
    StatusDescription = tostring(properties.status.description)
| extend 
    VulnerabilityLevel = case(
        todouble(MaxCVSS) >= 9.0, "🔴 Critical (9.0+)",
        todouble(MaxCVSS) >= 7.0, "🟠 High (7.0-8.9)",
        todouble(MaxCVSS) >= 4.0, "🟡 Medium (4.0-6.9)",
        todouble(MaxCVSS) >= 0.1, "🟢 Low (0.1-3.9)",
        Status == "NotApplicable", "⚪ Scanner Not Deployed",
        Status == "Healthy", "✅ No Vulnerabilities",
        "❓ Unknown"
    ),
    ScannerStatus = case(
        Status == "NotApplicable", "❌ Not Deployed",
        Status == "Healthy", "✅ Deployed & Clean", 
        Status == "Unhealthy", "⚠️ Deployed & Found Issues",
        "❓ Unknown"
    ),
    RiskDescription = case(
        todouble(MaxCVSS) >= 9.0, "Immediate patching required - Critical vulnerabilities detected",
        todouble(MaxCVSS) >= 7.0, "High priority patching recommended",
        todouble(MaxCVSS) >= 4.0, "Schedule patching in next maintenance window",
        Status == "NotApplicable", "Deploy vulnerability scanner to assess security posture",
        Status == "Healthy", "No action required - machine is secure",
        "Review scanner configuration"
    )
| project 
    MachineName = ResourceName,
    VulnerabilityLevel,
    MaxCVSSScore = MaxCVSS,
    ScannerStatus,
    RiskDescription,
    StatusDescription
content.title: 🛡️ Vulnerability Intelligence
content.description: Complete vulnerability analysis with security risk scores and clear action steps

content.json:
## 📊 **Security Health Summary**

**What You're Looking At**: A complete health check of your machine's security across all areas checked by Microsoft Defender.

**Why This Matters**: This gives you a simple report card for your machine's security. You'll see what percentage of security checks are passing and what needs attention.

### **📋 How to Read Your Security Report Card:**

**Security Score**: Think of this like a school grade - what percentage of security tests your machine passes
- **90%+** = Excellent (A grade) - Your security is fantastic
- **75-89%** = Good (B grade) - Pretty secure, minor improvements needed
- **60-74%** = Fair (C grade) - Some security problems to fix
- **Below 60%** = Poor (D/F grade) - Serious security issues requiring immediate attention

**Risk Profile**: The worst security problem found on your machine
- **🔴 High Risk** = Critical security issues found - fix immediately
- **🟡 Medium Risk** = Some security problems - fix soon
- **🟢 Low Risk** = Minor security issues - fix when convenient
- **✅ Secure** = No security problems found - well done!

**Assessment Categories**: Types of security checks performed (like different subjects in school)
- **Compute** = Computer security settings and patches
- **Networking** = Internet connection security
- **Data** = Information protection
- **Identity** = User access controls

### **💡 Business Example:**
If you see "75% Good" with "🟡 Medium Risk", this means your machine passes 75% of security tests (good grade) but has some medium-priority security issues that should be fixed in the next few weeks.

content.query:
// SECURITY POSTURE SUMMARY
// High-level security health metrics we can calculate
SecurityResources
| where type == "microsoft.security/assessments"
| extend 
    ResourceId = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceId),
    ResourceName = tostring(properties.resourceDetails.ResourceName)
| where ResourceId contains "hybridcompute"
| where ResourceName == "{SelectedMachine}"
| extend 
    AssessmentName = tostring(properties.displayName),
    Category = tostring(properties.metadata.categories[0]),
    Severity = tostring(properties.metadata.severity),
    Status = tostring(properties.status.code)
| summarize 
    TotalAssessments = count(),
    SecurityIssues = countif(Status == "Unhealthy"),
    CompliantItems = countif(Status == "Healthy"),
    HighRiskIssues = countif(Status == "Unhealthy" and Severity == "High"),
    MediumRiskIssues = countif(Status == "Unhealthy" and Severity == "Medium"),
    Categories = make_set(Category)
    by MachineName = ResourceName
| extend 
    SecurityScore = round((CompliantItems * 100.0) / TotalAssessments, 1),
    RiskProfile = case(
        HighRiskIssues > 0, "🔴 High Risk",
        MediumRiskIssues > 0, "🟡 Medium Risk", 
        SecurityIssues > 0, "🟢 Low Risk",
        "✅ Secure"
    )
| extend
    ComplianceLevel = case(
        SecurityScore >= 90, "✅ Excellent",
        SecurityScore >= 75, "🟢 Good",
        SecurityScore >= 60, "🟡 Fair",
        "🔴 Poor"
    )
| project 
    MachineName,
    SecurityScore = strcat(tostring(SecurityScore), "%"),
    ComplianceLevel,
    RiskProfile,
    TotalAssessments,
    CompliantItems,
    SecurityIssues,
    HighRiskIssues,
    MediumRiskIssues,
    CategoriesCovered = strcat_array(Categories, ", ")
content.title: 📊 Security Health Summary
content.description: Complete security report card showing compliance scores, risk levels, and assessment coverage

content.json:
## ⚙️ **Azure Arc Extensions & Management**

**What You're Looking At**: Azure Arc agent version status, installed extensions, and Azure Policy compliance for this machine.

**Why This Matters**: Make sure the Arc agent is up-to-date, check that required extensions are installed, and verify policy compliance for governance.

### **🏛️ Understanding Azure Policy Compliance:**

**What Azure Policy Is**: Think of Azure Policy as your organisation's "rules engine" for cloud resources. IT administrators create policies (rules) that automatically check if machines follow company standards.

**Common Policy Examples**:
- **🛡️ Security**: "All machines must have antivirus installed"
- **💾 Backup**: "All servers must have backup enabled"
- **🏷️ Tagging**: "All resources must be tagged with owner and cost centre"
- **🌍 Location**: "Resources can only be deployed in approved regions"
- **⚙️ Configuration**: "Windows machines must have specific security settings"

**How to Read the Compliance Tile**:
- **Big Number**: Percentage of policies this machine is following correctly
- **Small Number**: Count of policies this machine is violating
- **🟢 Green (95%+)**: Machine follows almost all company rules
- **🟡 Yellow (85-94%)**: Machine mostly compliant, minor issues
- **🟠 Orange (70-84%)**: Some policy violations need attention
- **🔴 Red (Below 70%)**: Machine has significant compliance problems
- **⚪ Grey**: No policies assigned to this machine

**💡 Your Example**: If you see a red tile with "Poor Compliance", a percentage like 25%, and the small number 17:
- **25%** = Only 25% of your organisation's IT policies are being followed
- **17** = There are exactly 17 specific policy violations that need to be fixed
- **🔴 Red + "Poor Compliance"** = This machine has serious governance issues requiring immediate attention

**Why This Matters**: Poor policy compliance can mean security risks, failed audits, or non-compliance with regulations. High compliance means your machine follows your organisation's governance standards.

content.query:
// Arc Agent Version Status - Simplified approach
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines'
| where name =~ '{SelectedMachine}'
| extend 
    machineName = name,
    currentVersion = tostring(properties.agentVersion),
    status = tostring(properties.status),
    lastStatusChange = todatetime(properties.lastStatusChange)
| extend 
    AgentStatus = case(
        status == "Connected", "🟢 Connected",
        status == "Disconnected", "🔴 Disconnected", 
        "⚪ Unknown"
    ),
    VersionInfo = currentVersion,
    DaysSinceUpdate = datetime_diff('day', now(), lastStatusChange)
| project machineName, AgentStatus, VersionInfo, currentVersion = VersionInfo, DaysSinceUpdate
content.title: 🔄 Azure Arc Agent Version Status
content.description: Current Arc agent version and update recommendations

content.query:
// Arc Extensions Status - Simplified approach
resources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.hybridcompute/machines/extensions'
| extend 
    machineName = tostring(split(id, '/')[8]),
    ExtensionName = name,
    ExtensionType = tostring(properties.type),
    Publisher = tostring(properties.publisher),
    Version = tostring(properties.typeHandlerVersion),
    ProvisioningState = tostring(properties.provisioningState),
    AutoUpgrade = tobool(properties.enableAutomaticUpgrade)
| where machineName =~ '{SelectedMachine}'
| extend 
    StatusIcon = case(
        ProvisioningState == "Succeeded", "🟢 Running",
        ProvisioningState == "Failed", "🔴 Failed",
        ProvisioningState == "Creating", "🟡 Installing",
        "⚪ Unknown"
    ),
    AutoUpgradeIcon = case(
        AutoUpgrade == true, "✅ Enabled",
        "❌ Disabled"
    )
| project ExtensionName, StatusIcon, ExtensionType, Publisher, Version, AutoUpgradeIcon
| order by ExtensionName asc
content.title: 🔧 Installed Arc Extensions
content.description: Azure Arc extensions providing monitoring, security, and management capabilities

content.query:
// Azure Policy Compliance Status - Fixed approach
policyresources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.policyinsights/policystates'
| extend 
    resourceName = tostring(split(properties.resourceId, '/')[8]),
    PolicyName = tostring(properties.policyDefinitionName),
    ComplianceState = tostring(properties.complianceState)
| where resourceName =~ '{SelectedMachine}'
| where ComplianceState in ("Compliant", "NonCompliant", "Conflict")
| summarize 
    TotalPolicies = count(),
    CompliantPolicies = countif(ComplianceState == "Compliant"),
    NonCompliantPolicies = countif(ComplianceState == "NonCompliant"),
    ConflictPolicies = countif(ComplianceState == "Conflict")
| extend 
    CompliancePercentage = case(
        TotalPolicies > 0, round(100.0 * CompliantPolicies / TotalPolicies, 1),
        0.0
    )
| extend 
    ComplianceStatus = case(
        TotalPolicies == 0, "⚪ No Policies",
        CompliancePercentage >= 95, "🟢 Excellent",
        CompliancePercentage >= 85, "🟡 Good",
        CompliancePercentage >= 70, "🟠 Needs Attention",
        "🔴 Poor Compliance"
    )
| project ComplianceStatus, CompliancePercentage, TotalPolicies, CompliantPolicies, NonCompliantPolicies
content.title: 📋 Azure Policy Compliance
content.description: Governance and compliance status against assigned Azure policies

content.query:
// Detailed Policy Violations - What are the specific non-compliant policies?
policyresources
| where type =~ 'microsoft.policyinsights/policystates'
| extend 
    resourceName = tostring(split(properties.resourceId, '/')[8]),
    PolicyName = tostring(properties.policyDefinitionName),
    PolicyDisplayName = tostring(properties.policyDefinitionDisplayName),
    ComplianceState = tostring(properties.complianceState),
    PolicyCategory = tostring(properties.policyDefinitionGroupNames[0]),
    ResourceType = tostring(properties.resourceType),
    Timestamp = todatetime(properties.timestamp)
| where resourceName =~ '{SelectedMachine}'
| where ComplianceState == "NonCompliant"
| extend 
    ViolationSeverity = case(
        PolicyDisplayName contains "security" or PolicyDisplayName contains "Security", "🔴 High - Security",
        PolicyDisplayName contains "backup" or PolicyDisplayName contains "Backup", "🟠 Medium - Backup",
        PolicyDisplayName contains "tag" or PolicyDisplayName contains "Tag", "🟡 Low - Tagging",
        PolicyDisplayName contains "location" or PolicyDisplayName contains "Location", "🟡 Low - Location",
        PolicyDisplayName contains "encryption" or PolicyDisplayName contains "Encryption", "🔴 High - Encryption",
        PolicyDisplayName contains "monitoring" or PolicyDisplayName contains "Monitoring", "🟠 Medium - Monitoring",
        "🔵 Standard"
    ),
    PolicySummary = case(
        strlen(PolicyDisplayName) > 80, strcat(substring(PolicyDisplayName, 0, 77), "..."),
        PolicyDisplayName
    ),
    DaysNonCompliant = datetime_diff('day', now(), Timestamp)
| project 
    PolicySummary,
    ViolationSeverity,
    ComplianceState,
    PolicyCategory,
    DaysNonCompliant,
    Timestamp
| order by ViolationSeverity desc, DaysNonCompliant desc
| take 20
content.title: 🚨 Specific Policy Violations - What Needs to Be Fixed
content.description: Detailed list of the specific policies this machine is violating, prioritised by severity and how long they've been non-compliant

content.json:
## 📊 **Operational Intelligence & Timeline**

**What You're Looking At**: Historical operational data including connection patterns, maintenance events, and system updates.

**Why This Matters**: Understand operational patterns, maintenance windows, and spot potential issues before they impact business operations.

content.query:
// Connection and Heartbeat History
Heartbeat
| where Computer == '{SelectedMachine}'
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| summarize 
    HeartbeatCount = count(),
    FirstHeartbeat = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastHeartbeat = max(TimeGenerated),
    UptimeHours = datetime_diff('hour', max(TimeGenerated), min(TimeGenerated))
    by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| extend 
    HealthScore = case(
        HeartbeatCount >= 1440, 100, // 24 hours * 60 minutes = full day
        HeartbeatCount >= 720, 75,   // Half day
        HeartbeatCount >= 360, 50,   // Quarter day
        25
    ),
    AvailabilityStatus = case(
        HeartbeatCount >= 1440, "🟢 Fully Available",
        HeartbeatCount >= 720, "🟡 Mostly Available",
        HeartbeatCount >= 360, "🟠 Intermittent",
        "🔴 Poor Connectivity"
    )
| project TimeGenerated, AvailabilityStatus, HealthScore, HeartbeatCount
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: 📡 Connectivity & Availability Timeline
content.description: Daily connectivity patterns and availability scores based on heartbeat data

content.json:
## 🎯 **Azure Arc Machine Intelligence Summary**

**🚀 Well done!** You've just experienced the complete power of Azure Arc machine management and monitoring.

### **✨ What You've Seen:**

**🔍 Complete Visibility:**
- Live machine health and performance metrics
- Historical trends and capacity planning insights
- Comprehensive security status with Defender integration

**🛡️ Advanced Security Intelligence:**
- **BREAKTHROUGH**: Real CVSS vulnerability scores and risk assessments
- Active threat detection and alert management
- Complete security health report cards with compliance scoring
- Software change tracking and inventory management

**💎 Enterprise Features:**
- SQL Server discovery and security assessment
- Azure Arc extensions management and monitoring
- Azure Policy compliance and governance oversight

**📊 Operational Excellence:**
- Connection and availability timeline
- Streamlined operational intelligence and monitoring
- End-to-end machine lifecycle management

### **🎬 Business Impact:**

This dashboard shows how **Azure Arc transforms any machine** into a fully managed, monitored, and secured asset in your hybrid cloud environment. With a single click, administrators and executives can understand everything about any machine in their infrastructure - including real vulnerability intelligence that was previously hidden.

**Key Benefits:**
- ✅ **Unified Management** - One screen for all machine intelligence
- ✅ **Live Insights** - Real-time performance and security data
- ✅ **Proactive Security** - Live vulnerability intelligence with CVSS risk scores
- ✅ **Compliance Automation** - Policy-driven governance at scale
- ✅ **Operational Efficiency** - Reduce time to resolution with comprehensive machine context

---

*This is the future of hybrid infrastructure management - delivered today through Azure Arc.*
